nodes.name: 澳大利亚
nodes.summary: 澳大利亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联221种鸟类，包括南方鶴鴕、鵲鵝、细斑树鸭等221种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 约翰·古尔德
nodes.summary: 约翰·古尔德是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联71种鸟类，包括绿棉凫、凍原豆雁、黄腹角雉等71种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 新南威爾士州
nodes.summary: 新南威爾士州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联35种鸟类，包括尖羽树鸭、叢塚雉、Black-eared cuckoo等35种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 北領地
nodes.summary: 北領地是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联25种鸟类，包括绿棉凫、Torresian imperial pigeon、白面鷺等25种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 南澳大利亚州
nodes.summary: 南澳大利亚州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联24种鸟类，包括Dusky moorhen、鹗、黄尾黑凤头鹦鹉等24种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 灌丛
nodes.summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

nodes.name: Cinnamon quail-thrush
nodes.summary: Cinnamon quail-thrush（Cinnamon quail-thrush，学名 Cinclosoma cinnamomeum），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联5个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括灌丛，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The cinnamon quail-thrush (Cinclosoma cinnamomeum) is a small to medium-sized species of bird that is endemic to Australia. This bird is found in the arid and semi-arid regions of central Australia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The cinnamon quail-thrush (Cinclosoma cinnamomeum) is a small to medium-sized species of bird that is endemic to Australia. This bird is found in the arid and semi-arid regions of central Australia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The cinnamon quail-thrush is an Australian endemic that is typically found in arid and semi-arid regions of central Australia. These regions are defined by the presence of desert vegetation and land forms as well as by low rainfall, with less than per annum in most areas. Its distribution size is estimated to be , spanning over southwest Queensland, northwest New South Wales, northeastern South Australia and the southeast of the Northern Territory. Within these areas the cinnamon quail-thrush has been found in grass and shrublands; however, it is more commonly found among dry stony areas, especially around dry creek lines

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: While the cinnamon quail-thrush's population has not been measured, it is thought that the species is slowly declining due to ongoing habitat degradation caused by livestock and introduced herbivores
